summary.intent: The user was evaluating whether to use `entire checkpoint rewind` via fzf and assessing whether the entire-fzf plugin was usable/correct.
summary.outcome: Determined that wrapping `entire checkpoint rewind` in fzf is unnecessary since it already has an interactive UI. A redundant error message in `etfc` was consolidated, committed, and the plugin was confirmed syntactically valid.

repo: entire-fzf.plugin.zsh is a zsh plugin wrapping the `entire` CLI tool with fzf-based helpers
`entire checkpoint rewind` already provides its own interactive UI, making an fzf wrapper redundant
_entire_current_session_id returns empty string (exit 0) when no session exists, and fails outright outside a git repo — these two failure modes were previously handled with duplicate error messages

code.finding: etfc had two separate error checks for session_id that produced identical error messages; consolidated into a single check since _entire_current_session_id covers both the non-git and no-session cases

workflow: Use `zsh -n <file>` to syntax-check a zsh plugin before evaluating logic
Before adding fzf wrappers, check if the underlying CLI already provides an interactive UI

open_items: Whether to use `entire checkpoint rewind` at all in the workflow (deferred — depends on whether reverting to a checkpoint becomes a needed use case)
